title: Overflow Tables
text: Overflow Tables DBMSs may impose upper limits to the number of fields in a table, the length of a field, and the length of a record. Some DBMSs do not support variable-length fields with a high maximum length. To overcome these limitations, Uniface provides an overflow mechanism, in which data that is represented as a single modeled entity is automatically split into two tables—the base table and an overflow table. For every record at the model level, Uniface creates one record in the base table and as many records as needed in the overflow table. This mechanism is automatically handled by Uniface. The connector does not need to know if a connector request is requested for a base table or for an overflow table. For a given record in the base table, when Uniface needs data from the overflow table, it fetches all of the records of the overflow table that have a primary key which corresponds to the record in the base table. The records are fetched from the overflow table in the order of segment number.
